Public Good Award First Time?: Yes, it's my first time submitting to this Award, but I've received SDF Infrastructure Grants before.
Retro Impact Past 3 Months:
Over the last quarter, we focused on ensuring the SDK stayed compatible with the latest network upgrades and on fixing key developer pain points:

- Protocol 23 support was added, so developers can seamlessly transition their applications when the protocol goes live.
- SubmitTransactionAsync bug fix ensured correct handling of Horizon’s HTTP 201 responses, preventing false exceptions.
- ClaimableBalanceId improvements enhanced usability when working with claimable balances.
- StrKey improvements increased reliability for address and key management.

These updates ensured that .NET developers can confidently build and run Stellar applications without service interruptions. GitHub activity, forks, and community engagement confirm active adoption in real-world projects, including production wallets like Beans.
Deliverables Past 3 Months:
'- Implemented Protocol 23 support (#81).
- Fixed SubmitTransactionAsync exception handling for Horizon’s HTTP 201 responses (#76).
- Improved ClaimableBalanceId usability (#78).
- Enhanced StrKey handling (#71).
All features and fixes are live in the main branch of the repository: Beans-BV/dotnet-stellar-sdk
Goals Upcoming 3 Months:
Note: Budget ask for quarter is $13,000, because our existing Infrastructure Grant is running out in November, the remainder of Q4 is supported by the SCF Public Goods Award with adjusted budget ($7,489).
Our main objective for Q4 is to modernize the SDK, expand protocol coverage, and improve test reliability and documentation:
- Transition from Json.NET to System.Text.Json for serialization.
- Add retry mechanism with default configuration for both Server and SorobanServer.
- Add support for SEP-6 (Deposit/Withdraw) and SEP-24 (Interactive Flows).
- Improve unit test coverage for key asset classes, converters, operations, request builders, and response result types.
- Provide advanced real-world Soroban contract invocation examples in the examples library.
- Refactor and improve response classes under StellarDotnetSdk.Responses:
    - Add XML documentation for all publicly visible properties.
    - Properly resolve “Non-nullable property '..' is uninitialized” warnings rather than disabling nullable checks.
Upcoming Deliverables 3 month:
'- Migration from Json.NET to System.Text.Json.
- Retry mechanism released with sensible defaults.
- SEP-6 and SEP-24 support included.
- Expanded test coverage for:
    - StellarDotnetSdk.Assets.* (Asset, ChangeTrustAsset, LiquidityPoolShareChangeTrustAsset, LiquidityPoolShareTrustlineAsset, TrustlineAsset).
    - StellarDotnetSdk.Converters.* (AssetJsonConverter, KeyPairJsonConverter, LinkJsonConverter).
    - StellarDotnetSdk.Operations.ChangeTrustOperation.
    - StellarDotnetSdk.Requests.* (EffectsRequestBuilder, OperationsRequestBuilder, PathsRequestBuilder, TransactionsRequestBuilder).
    - StellarDotnetSdk.Responses.Results.* (ExtendFootprintTtlResult, InvokeHostFunctionResult, LiquidityPoolDepositResult, LiquidityPoolWithdrawResult, ManageBuyOfferResult, RestoreFootprintResult).
- New Soroban contract invocation examples published in StellarDotnetSdk.Examples.Soroban.SorobanExamples.
- Refactored and documented response classes with resolved nullability warnings.

Retro Impact Past 3 Months:
The Flutter Stellar SDK has maintained full compatibility with the latest Stellar ecosystem updates, ensuring continued reliability for dependent applications. Key achievements include:

- **Protocol 23 Compatibility**: The SDK successfully integrated Protocol 23 support, preventing service disruptions for applications following the mainnet deployment.
- **Soroban Contract Bindings**: Implemented comprehensive support for Soroban contract bindings, expanding the SDK's smart contract interaction capabilities.
- **Developer Engagement**: The SDK has garnered 79 stars on GitHub, reflecting strong community interest and adoption within the Stellar ecosystem.
- **Active Ecosystem Integration**: The SDK powers multiple applications, including the Beans Wallet, Mykobo mobile app, Meru, Stack Wallet, PayQin, CodeLnPay, Bousol Wallet and the Flutter Wallet SDK.
Deliverables Past 3 Months:
'- **Protocol 23 Support**: Delivered in [Release 2.1.0](https://github.com/Soneso/stellar_flutter_sdk/releases/tag/2.1.0)
- **Contract Bindings Implementation**: 
 - Core functionality: [Release 2.1.3](https://github.com/Soneso/stellar_flutter_sdk/releases/tag/2.1.3)
 - Documentation: [SDK Soroban Documentation](https://github.com/Soneso/stellar_flutter_sdk/blob/master/soroban.md#contract-bindings)
 - Bindings generation: [Release 0.5.0b0](https://github.com/lightsail-network/stellar-contract-bindings/releases/tag/0.5.0b0)
 - Stellar CLI integration: [Release 23.1.1](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-cli/releases/tag/v23.1.1)
- **Ongoing Maintenance**: Continuous updates across releases 2.1.0 through 2.1.5
- **Community Engagement Infrastructure**: 
 - [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/Soneso/stellar_flutter_sdk/discussions)
 - [Community Feedback Form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd5bHI_Jvqi9GXNHYFAHLo1wFPNa0A6_CCdJXW9mzu4YODyyQ/viewform)
Goals Upcoming 3 Months:
The primary objectives for the upcoming quarter focus on maintaining SDK reliability, expanding Stellar Ecosystem Proposal (SEP) coverage, and enhancing Soroban functionality:

- **Continuous Compatibility**: Ensure seamless integration with Horizon, Soroban RPC, and all SEP updates as they are released.
- **Responsive Maintenance**: Provide timely responses to GitHub issues, feature requests, and Discord inquiries to support the developer community.
- **Technical Modernization**: Refactor legacy code to align with current standards, enhance test coverage and documentation quality.
- **Enhanced Soroban Integration**: Streamline developer experience when implementing Soroban-based features in applications.
Upcoming Deliverables 3 month:
**Continuous Maintenance**
Regular SDK updates addressing bug fixes, feature requests, code modernization, test coverage improvements, and documentation enhancements to ensure long-term sustainability.

**SEP-47 Implementation**
Extension of existing contract parser infrastructure to support [SEP-47](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/ecosystem/sep-0047.md), exposing identification of SEPs implemented by smart contracts..

**SEP-48 Implementation**
Building upon the recently added contract bindings support, full [SEP-48](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/ecosystem/sep-0048.md) support will provide robust contract event parsing capabilities. This deliverable will significantly simplify event data access for developers, complete with tests and documentation.

**SEP-45 (Preliminary Work)**
Initiate development of [SEP-45](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/ecosystem/sep-0045.md) support for Stellar Web Authentication with Contract Accounts. Given the complexity and scope of this specification, this work will be conducted in a separate development branch during Q4 2025, with planned completion in Q1 2026. This feature will be critical for enabling contract-based Stellar Web Authentication.
Goals Upcoming 3 Months (from Previous S:
'- Keep the SDK up to date, compatible with new protocol, SEP updates, Horizon, Soroban RPC and other releases. E.g., upcoming protocol 23
- Maintenance of the SDK, reply and work on reported GitHub issues and inquiries via Discord.
- Continue improving the Soroban support to be able to provide Smart contract bindings as already offered for JS, Rust, Java and Python
- Set up a process to gain deeper insights into the use of the SDK in the future, better assess the relevance of features, and identify ecosystem demand.


With the help of the goals listed above, the SDK will remain up to date and applications that use the SDK will not crash when protocol 23 is introduced, for example. Furthermore, Soroban support will be expanded with an important feature that has already been implemented by SDF and is already offered in various other SDKs, so that iOS developers can also benefit from it.

The only (very limited) evidence for continued usage that I have at this time is a traffic statistic from GitHub. For the next submission, I want to find as many projects as possible that use the SDK so that I can give you more detailed insights.

Retro Impact Past 3 Months:
Over the past three months, OpenGrants has focused on deploying foundational infrastructure to provide structured, ecosystem-wide funding data and analytics that better support Stellar grant management processes. You can refer to this recently published blog post for all the details on what’s new (and what’s next) for OpenGrants. These efforts have generated the following impact:

- Transformed and integrated all available data from 38 funding rounds of the Stellar Community Fund, totaling 716 applications and $49.1 million (data available at: https://github.com/metagov/oss-funding/blob/main/daoip-5/json/stellar/grants_pool.json) 
- Developed a comprehensive grant analytics dashboard enabling cross-system comparisons and ecosystem-wide insights, as well as a SCF profile page featuring funding trend analyses and an index of all SCF-funded projects
- Launched OpenGrants gateway API serving DAOIP-5-compliant data with unified query interface and real-time updates for Giveth and Octant API data, which include associated SCF funded rounds and funded projects
- Successfully integrated Giveth data pipeline into Open Source Observer (OSO) with an update to OSO’s GraphQL Script, enabling seamless integration of associated Stellar funding rounds
- Developed partnerships with multiple grant programs, including Giveth, Celo, Karma, and Gitcoin, further expanding our ecosystem funding data footprint

The OpenGrants deliverables of the last three months include: 

I. Tech Infrastructure Development 
- Live data transformation visible in API responses (proof: grants.daostar.org/dev)
- Completed full data ingestion, transformation, and structuring for 6 Grant Systems and 138 Grant Rounds, including the following: SCF (Rounds 1-38), Giveth, Octant, Celo Public Goods, Optimism, Arbitrum (proof: all data housed in the OSS Funding Repo)
- Audited all integrated datasets for DAOIP-5 field completeness and achieved core field coverage for Stellar SCF (97% compliance)
- Integrated KarmaGAP API for a Project ID check to serve as Project Identity Source
- Updated OpenGrants and DAOIP-5 integration documentation, and created schema mapping guides for grant system integration (proof: live API documentation and integration guides)

II. API Deployment 
- Completed partner API integrations with schema mapping and transformation
- Deployed OpenGrants API with real-time DAOIP-5-compliant data delivery (refer to our approach to data integration for details on data freshness and SLAs)
- Conducted end-to-end testing including load testing and data validation
- Proof: functioning API at production endpoint serving cross-system grant data (grants.daostar.org/dev), Stellar data available programmatically from this endpoint: https://daoip5.daostar.org/stellar 

III. Dashboard & Analytics 
- Grants analytics dashboard featuring ecosystem funding data and a SCF profile page (proof: grants.daostar.org, https://grants.daostar.org/systems/stellar )
Goals Upcoming 3 Months:
Goals of the next 3 months
In the next three months our focus is shifting from infrastructure building and delivery, to working with the Stellar ecosystem team to provide direct value through deeper process integration, advanced analytics and insights, and the first steps towards impact measurement. We plan to deliver the following impact to the Stellar ecosystem:  
- Valuable grant analytics: Increasing the visibility of the OpenGrants stack (DAOIP-5, data lake, API, and dashboard) within the Stellar ecosystem, establishing a documented feedback loop with team members and builders and publishing a “v1” grant system report
- Project performance tracking and data visualization: Building milestone funding and progression data into the DAOIP-5 schema, grant system reporting, and Stellar dashboard page (specifically requested by the SCF team)

Goals Upcoming 3 Months:
CoinFabrik has been actively researching the application of AI for vulnerability detection and clustering in smart contracts. Our work has shown that AI can complement traditional deterministic analysis by reasoning over patterns, data flows, and complex interactions that rule-based tools may miss, thereby reducing false negatives and providing explainable insights. Integrating these AI techniques with existing static analysis frameworks can significantly enhance the security assessment of Stellar smart contracts, enabling more reliable detection of vulnerabilities, faster audit cycles, and ultimately contributing to the robustness and trustworthiness of applications built on the Stellar network.

We are proposing for 2025-Q4 and following grants to progressively complete 20025-Q3 detectors while also advancing our AI initiative.

Month 1 – Add new detectors - Chunk 1
- Review historical and recent Soroban audit reports.
- Identify vulnerabilities that can be translated into static detection patterns.
- Engage with Stellar Audit Bank and Stellar Development Foundation security team to share insights and validate relevance of potential detectors.
- Design and implement at least 3 new detectors.

Month 2 – AI Research and Planning
- Survey the state of the art in static analysis and AI techniques applied to smart contracts (e.g., LLMs).
- Choose the most appropriate approach for the POC (Proof of Concept) based on the survey results.
- Select an initial set of smart contracts to use as test cases for validating the POC.

Month 3 – AI POC Design and Construction
- Design and build the POC based on the defined approach.
- Run the POC on the initial set of contracts to validate its functionality.
- Run the POC on an expanded set of contracts to evaluate scalability and effectiveness.

Month 1 – Add new detectors - Chunk 1

- Updated documentation for each detector, including detection logic and remediation advice.

Month 2 – AI Research and Planning

- Specification of the selected POC approach.
- Set of contracts chosen for initial testing.

Month 3 – AI POC Design and Construction
- Working POC code.
- Detailed POC design document.
- Results and findings document covering both sets of contracts.

This POC represents an initial step toward integrating AI capabilities into the Scout offering. The outcomes of this first stage will serve as a foundation for progressively embedding AI into Scout’s workflow, ultimately strengthening its security assessment capabilities and expanding the value delivered to Stellar developers and auditors.
